0.cn: 我学到了一个重要的教训。
0.ai_notes: 'learned a big lesson': to gain important knowledge from an experience.
0.remarks: 'learned a big lesson': 学到了深刻的教训，指从经历中获得了重要的经验。

1.ai_notes: 'replies': answers or responses to a message or question.
1.remarks: 'replies': 回复，指对消息或问题的回答。

2.original: One fellow reported that he got in trouble with the Bureau of Internal Revenue because he used a bargain accountant.
2.b1: One person said he had problems with the tax office because he chose a very cheap accountant.
2.cn: 有一个人说，他因为找了一个便宜的会计师，结果和税务局惹上了麻烦。
2.ai_notes: 'got in trouble': to be in a situation where you face punishment or problems. 'Bureau of Internal Revenue': the government agency responsible for collecting taxes. 'bargain accountant': a person who manages money and taxes for a very low price.
2.remarks:
'got in trouble': 惹上麻烦，指陷入可能面临惩罚或问题的境地。
'Bureau of Internal Revenue': 税务局，负责征税的政府机构。
'bargain accountant': 廉价会计，指收费极低但专业水平可能有限的会计。

3.original: Another went to a cut-rate doctor and later learned he had received a completely wrong diagnosis.
3.b1: Another person visited a cheap doctor and later found out that the doctor had given him a completely wrong medical opinion.
3.cn: 另一个人去看了一个收费很低的医生，后来发现医生给他的诊断完全是错的。
3.ai_notes: 'cut-rate': very cheap or low-priced. 'diagnosis': a doctor's identification of an illness or problem.
3.remarks:
'cut-rate': 廉价的，指价格非常低廉的。
'diagnosis': 诊断，指医生对疾病或问题的判断。

4.original: Others related the costs of going second class in home repairs, hotels, and other goods and services.
4.b1: Other people talked about how much it cost them when they chose cheaper options for things like fixing their homes, staying in hotels, and buying other products or services.
4.cn: 其他人则提到了在房屋维修、酒店以及其他商品和服务上选择次等品所付出的代价。
4.ai_notes: 'related': told a story or shared information. 'second class': lower quality or standard.
4.remarks:
'related': 讲述，指叙述故事或分享信息。
'second class': 二流的，指质量或标准较低的。

5.original: Of course, I've heard the argument many times, but I can't afford to go first class.
5.b1: I have often heard people say, "I can't afford to choose the best quality."
5.cn: 当然，我听过很多次这种说法：“我负担不起最好的。”
5.ai_notes: 'argument': a set of reasons given to support an idea. 'can't afford': to not have enough money to pay for something.
5.remarks:
'argument': 论点，指为支持某个观点而给出的一系列理由。
'can't afford': 负担不起，指没有足够的钱支付某物。

6.original: The simplest answer is, you cannot afford to go any other way.
6.b1: The simple truth is, you cannot afford to choose anything less than the best.
6.cn: 最简单的答案是，你负担不起选择其他任何方式。
6.ai_notes: 'go any other way': to choose a different, usually lower, standard.
6.remarks: 'go any other way': 选择其他方式，通常指选择较低的标准。

7.original: Certainly, in the long run, going first class actually costs you less than going second class.
7.b1: For sure, over a long time, choosing the best quality will actually save you money compared to choosing cheaper options.
7.cn: 当然，从长远来看，选择最好的实际上比选择次等的花费更少。
7.ai_notes: 'in the long run': over a long period of time, rather than just right now.
7.remarks: 'in the long run': 从长远来看，指跨越较长的时间段，而非仅仅关注当下。

8.original: Then, too, it's better to have fewer things and have quality than to have many things and have junk.
8.b1: Also, it's better to own fewer items that are good quality than to have many items that are bad quality.
8.cn: 此外，拥有少量优质的东西，也比拥有许多劣质的东西要好。
8.ai_notes: 'junk': things that are of very poor quality or useless.
8.remarks: 'junk': 垃圾，指质量极差或无用的东西。

9.original: It's better, for example, to have one really good pair of shoes than to have three pairs of second-class shoes.
9.b1: For example, it's better to have one pair of very good shoes than to have three pairs of cheap shoes.
9.cn: 举例来说，拥有一双真正好的鞋子，比拥有三双次等鞋子要好。
9.ai_notes: 'pair of shoes': two shoes that are worn together.
9.remarks: 'pair of shoes': 一双鞋，指成对穿着的两只鞋。

10.original: People rate you for quality, often subconsciously, perhaps.
10.b1: People judge you by the quality of what you do or have, often without even realizing it.
10.cn: 人们会根据你的品质来评价你，这通常是潜意识的。
10.ai_notes: 'rate': to judge or evaluate someone. 'subconsciously': doing something without being fully aware of it.
10.remarks:
'rate': 评价，指评估某人或某事。
'subconsciously': 潜意识地，指在未完全察觉的情况下做某事。

11.b1: Learn to recognize and choose good quality naturally.
11.ai_notes: 'instinct': a natural ability or feeling that helps you know what is right.
11.remarks: 'instinct': 直觉，指帮助你判断对错的天生能力或感觉。

12.original: It pays, and it costs no more, often costs less, than second-class.
12.b1: Choosing quality is worth it, and it often costs the same or even less than choosing cheaper options.
12.cn: 这样做是值得的，而且它并不比次等品贵，通常甚至更便宜。
12.ai_notes: 'it pays': it is a good idea or it brings benefits.
12.remarks: 'it pays': 是值得的，指某事是一个好主意或能带来好处。

13.original: Make your environment make you successful.
13.b1: Create an environment around you that helps you succeed.
13.ai_notes: 'environment': the surroundings or conditions in which you live and work.
13.remarks: 'environment': 环境，指你生活和工作的周围条件。

14.ai_notes: '1.': a marker used to start a list.
14.remarks: '1.': 列表标记，用于开启列表的第一项。

15.b1: Pay attention to your surroundings and how they affect you.
15.ai_notes: 'environment-conscious': being aware of how your surroundings influence your life.
15.remarks: 'environment-conscious': 环境意识，指意识到周围环境如何影响你的生活。

16.original: Just as body diet makes the body, mind diet makes the mind.
16.b1: Just like what you eat affects your body, what you think and experience affects your mind.
16.cn: 就像身体的饮食塑造身体一样，思想的饮食塑造思想。
16.ai_notes: 'mind diet': the information and thoughts you choose to fill your brain with.
16.remarks: 'mind diet': 精神食粮，指你选择用来充实大脑的信息和思想。

17.ai_notes: '2.': a marker used for the second point.
17.remarks: '2.': 列表标记，用于列表的第二项。

18.original: Make your environment work for you, not against you.
18.b1: Make sure your surroundings help you, instead of causing problems for you.
18.cn: 让你的环境为你服务，而不是与你作对。
18.ai_notes: 'work against you': to cause problems or make it harder for you to succeed.
18.remarks: 'work against you': 对你不利，指造成问题或让你更难成功。

19.original: Don't let suppressive forces—the negative, you-can't-do-it people—make you think defeat.
19.b1: Don't let negative people who say "you can't do it" make you feel like you will fail.
19.cn: 不要让压制性的力量——那些消极的、说“你做不到”的人——让你产生失败的想法。
19.ai_notes: 'suppressive forces': people or things that stop you from growing. 'defeat': the feeling of losing or failing.
19.remarks:
'suppressive forces': 压制力量，指阻碍你成长的人或事物。
'defeat': 失败，指输掉或未达目标的挫败感。

20.ai_notes: '3.': a marker used for the third point.
20.remarks: '3.': 列表标记，用于列表的第三项。

21.original: Don't let small-thinking people hold you back.
21.b1: Don't let people who have limited ideas or negative thoughts stop you from achieving your goals.
21.cn: 不要让思想狭隘的人阻碍你。
21.ai_notes: 'small-thinking': having limited or narrow ideas. 'hold you back': to stop you from making progress.
21.remarks:
'small-thinking': 思维狭隘，指想法有限或格局小。
'hold you back': 阻碍你，指阻止你取得进步。

22.original: Jealous people want to see you stumble.
22.b1: People who are jealous want to see you make mistakes or fail.
22.cn: 嫉妒你的人希望看到你跌倒。
22.ai_notes: 'stumble': to make a mistake or fail in your actions.
22.remarks: 'stumble': 跌倒/犯错，指在行动中犯错或受挫。

23.b1: Don't let them be happy by seeing you fail.
23.ai_notes: 'satisfaction': the feeling of pleasure when you get what you want.
23.remarks: 'satisfaction': 满足感，指得到想要的东西后产生的愉悦感。

24.original: Four, get your advice from successful people.
24.b1: Fourth, ask for advice from people who have already achieved success.
24.cn: 第四，向成功人士寻求建议。
24.ai_notes: 'advice': suggestions or guidance about what to do.
24.remarks: 'advice': 建议，指关于如何行动的指导或意见。

25.ai_notes: 'future': the time that is yet to come.
25.remarks: 'future': 未来，指尚未到来的时间。

26.original: Never risk it with freelance advisors who are living failures.
26.b1: Never put your future at risk by taking advice from independent advisors who are not successful themselves.
26.cn: 永远不要冒险听从那些自身生活失败的自由顾问的建议。
26.ai_notes: 'freelance advisors': people who give advice but are not part of a company. 'living failures': people who have not succeeded in their own lives.
26.remarks:
'freelance advisors': 自由顾问，指非公司雇员的咨询者。
'living failures': 生活中的失败者，指在自己的人生中未能取得成功的人。

27.original: Five, get plenty of psychological sunshine.
27.b1: Fifth, make sure you get a lot of positive mental energy.
27.cn: 第五，获得充足的心理阳光。
27.ai_notes: 'psychological sunshine': positive thoughts and happy experiences for your mind.
27.remarks: 'psychological sunshine': 心理阳光，指积极的思想和快乐的体验。

28.b1: Spend time with different groups of people.
28.ai_notes: 'circulate': to move around and meet different people.
28.remarks: 'circulate': 社交/流动，指四处走动并结识不同的人。

29.original: Discover new and stimulating things to do.
29.b1: Find new and exciting activities to try.
29.cn: 发现新的、令人兴奋的事情去做。
29.remarks: 'stimulating': 刺激的/有启发性的，指有趣且令人兴奋的。

30.original: Six, throw thought poison out of your environment.
30.b1: Sixth, remove negative thoughts and ideas from your surroundings.
30.cn: 第六，把思想毒素从你的环境中清除出去。
30.ai_notes: 'thought poison': negative or harmful ideas that hurt your progress.
30.remarks: 'thought poison': 思想毒素，指伤害你进步的负面或有害思想。

31.b1: Don't talk about other people's private lives or spread rumors.
31.ai_notes: 'gossip': talking about other people's private lives, often in a negative way.
31.remarks: 'gossip': 流言蜚语，指谈论他人的私生活，通常带有负面色彩。

32.original: Talk about people, but stay on the positive side.
32.b1: You can talk about people, but always say good things about them.
32.cn: 谈论他人时，要保持积极的一面。
32.ai_notes: 'stay on the positive side': to focus only on good things.
32.remarks: 'stay on the positive side': 保持积极，指只关注好的方面。

33.ai_notes: '7.': a marker used for the seventh point.
33.remarks: '7.': 列表标记，用于列表的第七项。

34.original: Go first class in everything you do.
34.b1: Always choose the best quality in everything you do.
34.cn: 在你做的每件事上都选择最好的。
34.ai_notes: 'go first class': to choose the best quality or standard in your actions.
34.remarks: 'go first class': 选择一流，指在行动中追求最高质量或标准。

35.original: You can't afford to go any other way.
35.b1: You cannot afford to choose anything less than the best.
35.cn: 你负担不起选择其他任何方式。
35.ai_notes: 'can't afford': to not have the luxury of choosing a lower standard because it will cost you more later.
35.remarks: 'can't afford': 负担不起（代价），指没有奢侈去选择低标准，因为那以后会付出更高代价。
